chapter_title.ar: باب الْمَرْأَةِ تُصَلِّي بِحِيَالِ الرَّجُلِ وَ الرَّجُلِ يُصَلِّي وَ الْمَرْأَةُ بِحِيَالِهِ
chapter_title.en: Chapter 15 – The woman prays Salāt parallel with the man, and the man prays Salāt and the woman is parallel to him

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: ضعيف على المشهور - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (15/72)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: ضعيف على المشهور - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (15/72)
text: الرَّجُلِ وَ الْمَرْأَةِ يُصَلِّيَانِ فِي وَقْتٍ وَاحِدٍ الْمَرْأَةُ عَنْ يَمِينِ الرَّجُلِ بِحِذَاهُ قَالَ لَا إِلَّا أَنْ يَكُونَ بَيْنَهُمَا شِبْرٌ أَوْ ذِرَاعٌ .

en.hubeali: Ali Bin Muhammad, from Sahl Bin Ziyad, from Ibn Sinan, from Ibn Muskan, from Abu Baseer,

(It has been narrated) from Abu Abdullahasws regarding the man and the woman both praying Salāt at one time, the woman being on the right of the man by his side. Heasws said: ‘No, unless if there happens to be one shibr (open palm’s width) or one cubit between the two of them’.
en.sarwar: 3. Ali ibn Muhammad has narrated from Sahl ibn Ziyad from ibn Sinan from ibn Muskan from abu Basir from who has said the following: “I once asked abu ‘Abd Allah, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, about a man and a woman performing Salah (prayer) at the same time and the woman is on his right parallel. He (the Imam) said, ‘No, unless there is a distance of one shibr or one yard between the two of them.’”
